content.rendered:
About Us

  • Peaceful, compact campsite on a working farm nestled in County Down’s drumlins
  • Only a 10‑minute drive to Drumkeeragh Forest and 30 minutes to Belfast
  • Shops and restaurants in Ballynahinch just 15 minutes away by car

Looking for a quiet getaway close to forests, beaches, and city life? Brook Cottage Camping, set among the rolling drumlins of County Down, is just the ticket. You’re only a 10‑minute drive from Drumkeeragh Forest—with its fantastic walking trails—and a 30‑minute drive from Belfast, the lively capital of Northern Ireland.

This small, family‑friendly pop‑up site on a working farm offers portable toilets and a dedicated washing‑up area. Tucked off a minor road, you’ll enjoy minimal traffic noise, yet it’s perfectly positioned for exploring Northern Ireland’s top sights.

Grab your fishing gear and head out to one of the nearby lakes—just a 15‑minute drive from the site—or stock up on essentials in the market town of Ballynahinch, where you’ll find the usual array of shops and eateries.

Take a 20‑minute drive through Dromara and Finnis to reach Slieve Croob, the birthplace of the River Lagan. After your ascent, you’ll be rewarded with sweeping vistas. And if you’re up for some indoor fun afterward, Lisburn—25 minutes away—offers a pool with water slides, a cinema, and a bowling alley.

Contact us
